title: lithocardium subcordatum
side_effects: Lithocardium subcordatum can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its high levels of anthraquinones. Long-term use can lead to the loss of essential electrolytes, resulting in muscle weakness, fatigue, and an irregular heartbeat. Additionally, an overdose of the plant can cause headaches, dizziness, and a rapid drop in blood pressure, which can be life-threatening in severe cases. It is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using Lithocardium subcordatum to avoid these side effects and ensure safe and effective treatment.
